short: A framework that treats AI text pipelines as programs and improves their prompts automatically.
definition: DSPy is an open-source framework, originating at Stanford, that lets developers build a chain of steps around a language model by describing each step's inputs and outputs rather than writing fixed instructions (prompts) by hand. It then automatically tries variations — including which example answers to show the model — to improve results against a chosen measure of success. This shifts effort from fiddling with fragile prompts to stating the desired behavior and goal.
